findings.description: Document layout is severely disrupted by misplaced header and footer elements appearing within the main text flow.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, the address footer and duplicated header text appear before the main 'PROCLAMATION' title, creating a large unnatural gap and disrupting the reading order.

findings.description: Document layout is disrupted by a misplaced footer element.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, the address footer is erroneously inserted between Section III and the subsequent paragraph.

findings.description: Document layout is disrupted by a misplaced footer element breaking a paragraph.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, the address footer is inserted mid-sentence, breaking the text between the end of item J and its continuation (the URL).
The graphical signature present in the PDF sign-off block is missing in the HTML, replaced by an empty line.

findings.description: Page headers and footers are inserted directly into the middle of continuous sentences at page breaks, which disrupts the text flow.
findings.evidence: In Section III.B, the text reads '...or more than 100 pounds of black sea bass from' followed immediately by the page footer, a header image, and then the rest of the sentence 'April 1 through December 31...'. A similar interruption occurs in Section IV.J where a URL is split across the page break.
